title: sliding_window.py brute-force function has no complexity comment
body: find_max_average_brute_force in patterns/python/sliding_window.py is a nice contrast to the optimized sliding-window version, but it's not labeled with its O(n*k) complexity anywhere, unlike some of the other pattern files that annotate each function. Would help learners understand exactly why the second function is the 'pattern' being taught.

title: Java file name inconsistent with the pattern name used elsewhere
body: Every other language's reverse-linked-list example is named ReverseList.* (ReverseList.cs, ReverseList.cpp, reverse_list.go, reverseList.js, reverse_list.py, reverseList.ts), but the Java version is named ReverseLinkedList.java. Small thing, but it breaks the otherwise consistent naming pattern across the patterns/ directory and made it harder to find when comparing implementations.

title: Go two-pointers example has no runnable usage sample
body: patterns/go/two_pointers.go defines the pattern functions but, unlike the Python version's docstring-style examples, there's no main()/example usage showing how to call it with sample input and expected output. Would make the pattern easier to try out directly with `go run`.

title: TypeScript monotonic-stack example uses any[] throughout
body: patterns/typescript/monotonicStack.ts types its inputs/outputs as `any[]` rather than `number[]` (or a generic), which undercuts the point of showing the pattern in TypeScript versus JavaScript. Since this repo is meant as a learning resource, proper typing would make it a better reference.

title: C# fast/slow pointers example missing explanatory comments
body: patterns/c#/FastAndSlowPointers.cs has the working implementation but no comments explaining the slow/fast pointer invariant (why fast moves 2x), while several other language versions of the same pattern include at least a short inline explanation. Could use a comment pass for consistency across languages.
